investigations.report_markdown:
## Lane B2 — Focus detection on macOS (Rust): findings & recommendation

All claims below are tagged VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE (live Swift/CLI on this Mac, macOS 26.5.1, build 25F80) or DOCUMENTED (crate/Apple docs). I used Swift to probe the exact C APIs the Rust crates wrap (objc2/accessibility-sys are thin FFI over the same symbols), so the live results transfer directly.

### 1) Frontmost app — easy, zero-permission (Rust)

`NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication` gives bundle id + name + pid with **no TCC prompt**.

- VERIFIED: Swift `NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication` → `bundleId=com.anthropic.claudefordesktop name=Claude pid=97977`. `lsappinfo front` agreed. No prompt.
- Rust crate: **objc2-app-kit** exposes `NSWorkspace::sharedWorkspace().frontmostApplication()` → `NSRunningApplication` with `bundleIdentifier()`, `localizedName()`, `processIdentifier()`. For push-realtime focus changes, observe `NSWorkspaceDidActivateApplicationNotification` (no polling).
  - docs: https://docs.rs/objc2-app-kit/latest/objc2_app_kit/struct.NSWorkspace.html
- Higher-level option: the **frontmost** crate (https://crates.io/crates/frontmost) wraps exactly this (objc2 + NSWorkspace notifications). Thin enough that depending directly on objc2-app-kit is fine and avoids a small extra dep.

Minimal snippet (objc2-app-kit ≥ 0.2):
```rust
use objc2_app_kit::NSWorkspace;
use objc2_foundation::NSString;

fn frontmost() -> Option<(String, String)> {
    unsafe {
        let ws = NSWorkspace::sharedWorkspace();
        let app = ws.frontmostApplication()?;
        let bid = app.bundleIdentifier().map(|s| s.to_string());
        let name = app.localizedName().map(|s| s.to_string());
        Some((bid.unwrap_or_default(), name.unwrap_or_default()))
    }
}
```
This alone gets you the RP `large_image`/`large_text` ("Working in Claude.app" vs "Claude Code in iTerm2") with no permissions.

### 2) Window TITLE is the hard part — and it's permission-gated

The title is what would carry project/branch/session, but it is NOT free:

- **CGWindowList path is blocked without Screen Recording.** VERIFIED: `CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess()=false` on this machine, and every layer-0 application window came back `name=<nil>` (Claude and Discord). Only Window Server menubar elements (layer 2147483630) had names. This is the documented behavior of `active-win-pos-rs` too: "title always returns an empty string unless you enable Screen Recording" (README, v0.11.0). Crates affected the same way: **active-win-pos-rs**, **x-win**, anything built on `CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo` / ScreenCaptureKit.
  - https://github.com/dimusic/active-win-pos-rs , https://crates.io/crates/x-win
- **Accessibility (AX) path reads titles WITHOUT Screen Recording.** VERIFIED: with `AXIsProcessTrusted()=true`, `AXUIElementCreateApplication(pid)` → `kAXFocusedWindowAttribute` → `kAXTitleAttribute` returned `'Claude'` for the very window CGWindowList couldn't name. So AX is the right tool for titles, at the cost of the **Accessibility** permission (one-time prompt, not Screen Recording).
  - Rust crates: **axuielement** v0.9.1 (2026-06-06) — covers `AXUIElementCreateApplication`, `AXTitle` string attribute, `AXObserver` for focus-change notifications, and `ProcessTrust`/`AXIsProcessTrusted`. https://lib.rs/crates/axuielement . Lower-level: **accessibility-sys** + **macos-accessibility-client** (for the trust check/prompt helper).

**Terminal scenario (the realistic shipping case if the user runs `claude` in a terminal):**
- iTerm2/Terminal/Ghostty typically set the window/tab title to the cwd (or `user@host: dir`) when the shell emits OSC 7 / sets `PROMPT`. You read that title via AX of the frontmost terminal (kAXFocusedWindowAttribute → kAXTitle, or per-tab via the AX children).
- tty→process mapping: for a terminal-hosted claude, `ps -o tty` gives the tty, and you can correlate the frontmost terminal tab's tty to the claude PID, then PID→cwd via lsof. **Fragility:** title-as-cwd depends on user shell config (not guaranteed); per-tab AX titles vary by terminal; and it needs Accessibility permission. Treat as a best-effort enrichment, not the core signal.
- IMPORTANT on this machine: the two live claude sessions have **tty `??` (none)** because Claude.app spawned them — so the tty path does not apply to the current Claude.app-hosted setup at all.

### 3) Claude.app-hosted sessions — the ceiling (be honest)

The current setup runs sessions *inside* Claude.app (Electron). VERIFIED ceiling:
- Frontmost resolves to `com.anthropic.claudefordesktop` / "Claude" — you know Claude.app is front, nothing more.
- AX tree of Claude.app: a single `AXWindow` titled "Claude" + 8 empty `AXGroup`s; a 12-level recursive walk found no tab/session/project label (Electron doesn't surface web a11y to AX unless explicitly enabled). So **you cannot determine which session/tab is focused via AX or window title.**
- Local state files don't help directly: `window-state.json` is geometry only; `bridge-state.json` maps remote↔local session UUIDs but carries no "focused" flag. (Digging Claude.app's leveldb Session Storage for a focused-session id is undocumented, likely locked while running, and will break on updates — not recommended.)

Bottom line for the in-app case: the best you can truthfully show is "N Claude Code sessions running, M projects" + which session is *most recently active* (next section). You cannot ship reliable per-tab focus for Claude.app.

### 4) Permissions matrix

| Approach | Permission | Prompt? | Gives you |
|---|---|---|---|
| NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication | none | no | front app bundle id/name/pid |
| pgrep + lsof (PID→cwd, session count) | none | no | which projects have running sessions, how many |
| Transcript mtime ranking | none (reads ~/.claude) | no | most-recently-active session/project |
| AX window title (AXUIElement) | Accessibility | yes, one-time | focused window title (terminal cwd, etc.) |
| CGWindowList / ScreenCaptureKit titles | Screen Recording | yes, one-time | window titles incl. background windows |

Prompt-avoiding design: stay entirely in rows 1–3 → a zero-permission product. Add AX (row 4) only as an opt-in "richer focus" upgrade; never require Screen Recording.

### 5) Recommendation — the signal to actually ship

Use a **layered focus resolver**, picking the highest-confidence source available:

1. **Primary (zero-permission, always works): most-recently-active session from transcript mtimes.** VERIFIED live: the newest `~/.claude/projects/**/**.jsonl` mtime tracks the current second during active work; sorting all transcripts by mtime reliably surfaces the session being worked on right now. Combine with `pgrep -f claude` (session count) and `lsof -a -p PID -d cwd` (PID→project) to know *how many* sessions run and *which projects*.
   - Two gotchas to code around: (a) raw mtime can point at a **subagent** `agent-*.jsonl` or a background workflow agent — filter to top-level session transcripts (or weight by which PID's cwd matches) so a long orchestration doesn't masquerade as "focus"; (b) don't reconstruct the project slug from cwd by naive `/`→`-` (dots/worktrees break it) — instead read the `cwd` field stored *inside* each transcript JSON, which is authoritative.
2. **Upgrade when frontmost is a known terminal AND Accessibility is granted:** read the terminal's focused-window/tab AX title (axuielement), parse cwd, and correlate to a running claude PID. This turns "most-recently-active" into "actually-focused" for terminal users. Best-effort; degrade silently if title lacks cwd.
3. **Frontmost app (always, zero-permission):** even without title, knowing `frontmost == Claude.app` vs `== iTerm2` vs `== something else` lets you set RP state like "actively in Claude.app" vs "session running in background," which materially improves the presence.

Net: ship a zero-permission core (mtime recency + lsof + frontmost bundle id) that already answers "how many sessions, which projects, which is active," and offer Accessibility as an optional toggle for true terminal focus. Do not promise per-tab focus inside Claude.app — it's not obtainable.

Sources:
- https://docs.rs/objc2-app-kit/latest/objc2_app_kit/struct.NSWorkspace.html
- https://crates.io/crates/frontmost
- https://github.com/dimusic/active-win-pos-rs
- https://crates.io/crates/x-win
- https://lib.rs/crates/axuielement
- https://docs.rs/macos-accessibility-client/latest/src/macos_accessibility_client/lib.rs.html
- https://developer.apple.com/forums/thread/794253 (AXIsProcessTrusted true but calls fail)
- Apple: CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess / Screen Recording — https://support.apple.com/en-ca/guide/mac-help/mchld6aa7d23/mac

investigations.area: LANE B3 — Headline metric: count of running Claude Code agents/sessions + active-vs-idle detection
investigations.feasibility_summary: Fully feasible and the single most reliable signal in this whole project. There is a purpose-built, machine-readable registry at ~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json that maps every live top-level Claude Code session: PID -> sessionId -> cwd -> startedAt -> entrypoint. Verified live: exactly 2 files (98608.json, 98863.json), both PIDs confirmed alive via kill(pid,0), and exactly 2 engine processes exist (the Codex-style "N agents" number is trivially and reliably derivable). Active-vs-idle per session is reliably derived from the session's JSONL transcript tail using a pending-tool-call heuristic (count of tool_use ids with no matching tool_result) plus transcript mtime age — validated correct on both live sessions right now (one BUSY awaiting assistant, one IDLE assistant-done). Subagents (Task/Explore agents) are NOT separate OS processes — they run in-process and instead surface as ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl (isSidechain:true) with a sidecar .meta.json {agentType, description}, so live subagent count = number of recently-active sidechain transcript files. Recommended Discord party.size = [busy_sessions, total_live_sessions].

key_facts.claim: ~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json is a live registry of running top-level CC sessions: one file per running engine PID, containing pid, sessionId, cwd, startedAt, procStart, version, entrypoint, kind. This is the authoritative source for the 'N agents running' metric.
key_facts.evidence: ls ~/.claude/sessions/ -> 98608.json, 98863.json. cat 98608.json -> {"pid":98608,"sessionId":"27c2524d-6f9b-4d16-a833-57f3fdaa68f7","cwd":"/Users/jakubsladek/Projects/private/fnetflyx","startedAt":1781987269616,"version":"2.1.181","kind":"interactive","entrypoint":"claude-desktop"}

key_facts.claim: Number of live top-level sessions = number of sessions/<PID>.json files whose PID is still alive. Verified live: 2 files, both PIDs alive, and exactly 2 'MacOS/claude' engine processes (excluding the disclaimer wrapper) exist. The numbers agree.
key_facts.evidence: ps engine-process count = 2; python os.kill(98608,0) and os.kill(98863,0) both return alive=True; both have a matching sessions/<pid>.json.

key_facts.claim: CRITICAL: the resident/active sessionId is NOT the --resume flag on the command line. PID 98608's argv shows --resume ff489433..., but sessions/98608.json reports the actual live sessionId as 27c2524d... (a forked session, --fork-session). The transcript being actively written (mtime ~now) is 27c2524d, confirming sessions/<PID>.json is correct and argv is stale.
key_facts.evidence: argv: '--resume ff489433-f9eb-4b5b-87b3-a4cb78c2b424 ... --fork-session'. sessions/98608.json sessionId=27c2524d. Active transcript 27c2524d.jsonl mtime=22:57 (current); ff489433.jsonl mtime=22:27 (stale).

key_facts.claim: Subagents (Task tool / Explore / Workflow agents) are NOT separate OS processes. They run in-process inside the parent engine. Direct children of the live engine PIDs are only the Playwright MCP server (npm exec @playwright/mcp) and a shell snapshot — no extra 'claude' engine.
key_facts.evidence: pgrep -P 98608 -> 98616 npm exec @playwright/mcp@latest. pgrep -P 98863 -> 4804 /bin/zsh snapshot, 98910 npm exec @playwright/mcp. Engine count stayed at 2 throughout. No child 'MacOS/claude' processes.

key_facts.claim: Subagents surface on disk as ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl with isSidechain:true, accompanied by agent-<id>.meta.json containing {agentType, description}. 653 sidechain transcript lines exist across the machine's history.
key_facts.evidence: ls .../1fa4afc7.../subagents/ -> agent-af1c350c38da99ed6.jsonl + .meta.json. meta.json content: {"agentType":"Explore","description":"Frontend security audit (SvelteKit)"}. First line keys include agentId, isSidechain:true, sessionId(parent), slug, gitBranch.

key_facts.claim: Active-vs-idle per session is reliably computed from the transcript tail by counting pending tool calls (tool_use ids minus tool_result tool_use_ids). pending>0 => BUSY (mid-tool); else if last message is assistant text => IDLE; else if last message is user => BUSY (awaiting assistant). Validated correct live on both sessions, and the mid-tool path validated by slicing a transcript right after a tool_use (pending=1 => BUSY).
key_facts.evidence: Live join: PID 98608 (27c2524d) -> BUSY(awaiting assistant), tr_age 61.5s. PID 98863 (d4f690f4) -> IDLE(assistant done), tr_age 361.8s. Slice test: 'Sliced at line 12 just after an assistant tool_use. pending tool calls = 1 => BUSY(mid-tool)'.

key_facts.claim: Transcript files have trailing non-message records (type=last-prompt, ai-title, mode) after the real conversation. The active/idle heuristic MUST filter to type in {user, assistant} with a dict message; a naive 'read the literal last line' approach is wrong because the last physical line is often 'mode' or 'ai-title', not a message.
key_facts.evidence: tail of 27c2524d ends: ...tool_result; then type=last-prompt; type=ai-title; type=mode (no message body). Filtering to user/assistant gives the true conversational tail.

risks: sessions/<PID>.json is an undocumented internal file of CC v2.1.181; its schema (keys pid/sessionId/cwd/startedAt/entrypoint) could change in a future CLI version. Mitigation: treat it as best-effort, fall back to ps-scan of 'MacOS/claude' engine processes for the raw count, and version-gate parsing.
Stale session files: if an engine crashes, its sessions/<PID>.json may linger. MUST validate liveness with os.kill(pid,0) (verified working) before counting; do not trust file existence alone.

argv --resume / --fork-session is misleading — using the command-line sessionId instead of sessions/<PID>.json would point at the wrong (stale) transcript. Verified live: PID 98608 argv says ff489433 but real session is 27c2524d. Always read sessions/<PID>.json.

Subagent live-count is heuristic: subagents are in-process with no PID and no liveness flag in the .jsonl. 'Currently running' subagents must be inferred from sidechain transcript mtime recency (e.g. written in last N seconds) under the active sessionId, plus the parent's last tool_use being a Task with no tool_result yet. This is softer than the top-level count.
Transcript tail ordering: trailing last-prompt/ai-title/mode records mean a naive last-line read is wrong. The heuristic must filter to user/assistant message records (verified).

Busy heuristic blind spot: a session waiting on a permission prompt or on user input looks like 'user turn / awaiting assistant' or 'idle'; pure transcript analysis can't distinguish 'thinking' from 'blocked on human'. Combining with process CPU (98863 showed ~11-21% CPU while busy vs ~1.5% idle) or a Stop/PreToolUse hook pair would disambiguate.
Reading another session's transcript while it is being appended: read the tail with a bounded byte read and tolerate a truncated final JSON line (already handled by try/except per line).
open_questions: Does CC emit a hook event the app could subscribe to instead of polling? A PreToolUse + Stop hook pair (configured in settings.json) would give push-realtime busy/idle and exact subagent start/stop, far better than mtime polling. Worth a dedicated lane: can hooks write to a unix socket/file the RP daemon tails?

Is there a documented/stable way to detect the FOCUSED session (which one the user is looking at in Claude.app)? B3 gives per-session busy + project; focus selection likely needs the desktop app's window/IPC state (separate lane).

For subagents, is there a more authoritative live signal than sidechain-file mtime — e.g. does the parent transcript log a Task tool_use whose tool_result is still pending (in-process equivalent of the busy heuristic)? Worth confirming on a session that actively spawns Task agents (the 2 live sessions did not use Task during the window observed; one used a Workflow tool).
Across multiple Claude.app desktop windows / multiple users, do all sessions write to the same ~/.claude/sessions/? Assumed yes (single user home); not tested with concurrent desktop apps.

## Lane B3 — "How many agents are running" + active-vs-idle (LIVE, verified on this Mac)

### TL;DR
This is the cleanest, most reliable metric in the project. macOS CC v2.1.181 maintains a live session registry at `~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json`. The agent count is simply **the number of those files whose PID is still alive**. Active-vs-idle comes from each session's transcript tail. Right now, live, the app would correctly display **2 sessions, 1 busy, 1 idle** -> Discord `party.size = [1, 2]`.

### 1) Defining the metric

Three distinct concepts — keep them separate:

| Concept | Definition | How to count (verified) |
|---|---|---|
| **(a) Top-level sessions** | Independent CC engine processes (one per Claude.app conversation/CLI invocation) | Count of `~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json` files with `os.kill(pid,0)` alive. Cross-check: `ps` count of `…/MacOS/claude` engine procs (exclude the `…/Helpers/disclaimer` wrapper). Both = **2** right now. |
| **(b) Subagents** | Task/Explore/Workflow agents spawned *inside* a session | **In-process, not OS processes.** Observable as `projects/<slug>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl` (`isSidechain:true`) + `agent-<id>.meta.json {agentType, description}`. Live count ≈ sidechain files with recent mtime under the active session. |
| **(c) In-session tasks** | The TODO checklist (TaskCreate/TaskUpdate) | `~/.claude/tasks/<sessionId>/<n>.json` with `status`. This is a *checklist*, not running agents — use only as a secondary "tasks N/M" stat. |

**The headline number the app should show = (a), the count of live top-level sessions.** It is exact, cheap, and self-cleaning (via liveness check). Optionally enrich with live subagent count from (b).

### 2) The authoritative source — `~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json`

```jsonc
// ~/.claude/sessions/98608.json  (verified content)
{ "pid":98608, "sessionId":"27c2524d-6f9b-4d16-a833-57f3fdaa68f7",
  "cwd":"/Users/jakubsladek/Projects/private/fnetflyx",
  "startedAt":1781987269616, "procStart":"Sat Jun 20 20:27:49 2026",
  "version":"2.1.181", "kind":"interactive", "entrypoint":"claude-desktop" }
```

This single file gives PID, **the true active sessionId**, cwd (project), start time (for the elapsed timer), and entrypoint. **Do not parse the process `argv`** for the sessionId: PID 98608's command line says `--resume ff489433… --fork-session`, but the *actual live* session is `27c2524d…` (the fork), as the registry correctly reports and as transcript mtimes confirm (`27c2524d.jsonl` is the file being written *now*; `ff489433.jsonl` is stale at 22:27).

### 3) Active-vs-idle — the pending-tool-call heuristic (validated)

For each live session: locate its transcript via `glob ~/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl`, read the tail, **filter to records with `type ∈ {user, assistant}` and a dict `message`** (the physical last lines are often `last-prompt`/`ai-title`/`mode` metadata — ignore them), then:

```python
pending = {tool_use ids} - {tool_result tool_use_ids}
if pending:                              state = "BUSY (mid-tool)"
elif last_role=="assistant" and last has text:  state = "IDLE (assistant done)"
elif last_role=="user":                  state = "BUSY (awaiting assistant)"
```

**Live result, both sessions, right now:**

```
    PID alive sessionId      state                    tr_age_s  cwd
  98608 True  27c2524d…       BUSY(awaiting assistant)   61.5    …/private/fnetflyx
  98863 True  d4f690f4…       IDLE(assistant done)      361.8    …/private
```

Both correct. The `BUSY(mid-tool)` branch is validated by slicing a live transcript immediately after an assistant `tool_use` -> `pending = 1` -> BUSY. Augment with transcript **mtime age** (idle sessions go quiet) and optionally **process CPU** (live `ps` showed the busy engine at 11–21% vs the idle one ~1.5%) to harden the signal. For push-realtime accuracy, a `PreToolUse`+`Stop` hook pair would be strictly better than polling (open question / separate lane).

### 4) Subagents in detail

- **Not processes**: `pgrep -P 98608/98863` returns only the Playwright MCP (`npm exec @playwright/mcp`) and a shell-snapshot zsh; engine count stays at 2.
- **On disk**: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl` with `isSidechain:true`, every line carrying `agentId, parentUuid, sessionId(parent), slug, gitBranch, cwd`. Sidecar `agent-<id>.meta.json` = `{"agentType":"Explore","description":"Frontend security audit (SvelteKit)"}` — perfect human-readable labels for the RP state line.
- **Live count** = sidechain transcript files under the *active* sessionId with mtime within the last few seconds; corroborate by checking the parent transcript for a `Task` `tool_use` whose `tool_result` is still pending (same heuristic as §3). Neither of the 2 currently-live sessions was running a Task subagent during the observation window (`Task` count = 0 in `27c2524d`; the other used a `Workflow` tool), so subagent live-count remains a best-effort file/heuristic signal rather than a hard process count.

### 5) Recommended real-time definition for the app

```
live_sessions  = [ s for s in sessions/*.json if os.kill(s.pid,0) ]        # exact
busy_sessions  = [ s for s in live_sessions if busy(transcript(s.sessionId)) ]
live_subagents = count of subagents/agent-*.jsonl with fresh mtime under any live session
```

**Discord mapping:** `party.size = [len(busy_sessions), len(live_sessions)]` -> reads "1 of 2". `timestamps.start` from the focused session's `startedAt/1000`. `details` = `Running <lastToolName> - <cwd basename> (<gitBranch>)` (cwd + gitBranch are on every transcript record — verified `"gitBranch":"feat/ui-ux-redesign"`). `state` aggregate = e.g. `2 sessions · 1 active · N subagents`. `small_image` = green/grey dot from the busy flag.

Poll cost is negligible (a couple of tiny JSON files + tail of a couple JSONL files), so 1–2s polling is fine; upgrade to hooks for push-realtime later.

investigations.report_markdown:
## Lane C1 — Discord Rich Presence: protocol, schema, and Rust ecosystem

### Bottom line
Everything the user wants is achievable through Discord's **local IPC Rich Presence** path, and I proved it works on this exact Mac (handshake + SET_ACTIVITY + clear all succeeded against the running Discord). No Discord SDK download, no app verification, no asset uploads required. The recommended implementation crate is **`discord-rich-presence` v1.1.0**.

---

### 1. Local IPC protocol (VERIFIED ON THIS MACHINE)

- **Socket:** unix domain socket `discord-ipc-0` … `discord-ipc-9` in (first set of) `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`, `$TMPDIR`, `$TMP`, `$TEMP`, else `/tmp`. On this Mac it is `/var/folders/3y/gmkcmkk1459813sgvwl32q700000gn/T/discord-ipc-0` (confirmed `s` socket type). Try `-0` through `-9` to support multiple Discord installs.
- **Framing:** 8-byte header = `op: u32 LE` + `length: u32 LE`, then `length` bytes of UTF-8 JSON. My Python test used `struct.pack("<II", op, len(json))` and Discord accepted it.
- **Opcodes:** `0` HANDSHAKE, `1` FRAME, `2` CLOSE, `3` PING, `4` PONG.
- **Handshake:** send op `0` with `{"v": 1, "client_id": "<app id>"}`. Discord replies with an op `1` FRAME, `cmd: "DISPATCH"`, `evt: "READY"`, `data: {config, user, v}`. (Confirmed live; user object redacted — I only printed key names.)
- **Set activity:** send op `1` with:
  ```json
  {"cmd":"SET_ACTIVITY","nonce":"<uuid>","args":{"pid":<os pid>,"activity":{...}}}
  ```
  `pid` is required for the IPC transport. `activity: null` clears presence (I used this to clean up). Discord **echoes every command back** as the response frame — use this lock-step to avoid flooding.
- **Error frames:** an op `1` FRAME with `evt: "ERROR"` and `data: {code, message}`. My successful frame had no `evt` and echoed `cmd: "SET_ACTIVITY"`.

### 2. Activity object — fields & exact limits (DOCUMENTED, key ones verified)

| Field | Limit | Use here |
|---|---|---|
| `details` | **max 128** | "Running command cargo check - private (master)" |
| `state` | **max 128** | "Opus 4.8 | Pro - $0.98 - 837.7K tok - Ctx 33%" |
| `details_url` / `state_url` | max 256 | click-through links (optional) |
| `status_display_type` | int: NAME=0, STATE=1, DETAILS=2 | choose what shows in compact member list |
| `timestamps.start` / `.end` | ms Unix ts | `start` alone → elapsed timer (0:06); `end` → countdown |
| `assets.large_image` / `small_image` | **max 313** | asset key **or external https URL** |
| `assets.large_text` / `small_text` | max 128 | hover text |
| `assets.large_url` / `small_url` | max 256 | clickable image |
| `party.id` | max 128 | stable id, e.g. "claude-sessions" |
| `party.size` | `[current, max]` (two ints) | **renders "X of Y" — the agent counter** |
| `buttons` | **max 2**, each `{label, url}` | optional extras |
| `type` | int (Playing/Listening/Watching/Competing) | leave default Playing |

`party.size` rendering is officially documented as "In a group (2 of 3)" and I confirmed `[2,4]` round-trips through SET_ACTIVITY. This is the user's "how many agents running" indicator.

### 3. Assets / images
- **External https URLs work** — no Art Asset upload needed. `large_image`/`small_image` (and `details`/`state` via `*_url`) accept any `http(s)://` URL ≤ 313 chars. External URLs additionally support GIF/animated WebP/AVIF (uploaded assets do not). Simplest path: host a Claude logo and reference its URL, or upload one asset key named e.g. `claude` in the app's Art Assets tab.
- Other encodings: `mp:<id>` (media proxy → media.discordapp.net), `twitch:`, `youtube:`, `spotify:` prefixes. We only need plain URL or an asset key.

### 4. Application / Client ID
- Create one **free** Discord application at the Developer Portal. The **Application ID = client_id**, is public, and is fine to ship hardcoded in the binary.
- **The application NAME is the header label** ("Playing <Name>") → name it **"Claude Code"** to mirror the Codex screenshot's "Codex App".
- **No verification or review** is needed for Rich Presence. No redirect URI / description / icon required to create the app (though you'll want an icon + one Art Asset for the badge).

### 5. Rate limits & reconnection
- **Updates:** Discord throttles activity updates. The public docs are inconsistent (older `discord-rpc` says 1/15s; the widely-observed real limit is ~**5 updates / 20s**), and hammering it (e.g. 10 updates in 5s) can *blank* the presence. Practical rule: **coalesce and push at most one update every ~2–4s**, only when something actually changed, and rely on the command-echo lock-step. The elapsed timer needs no per-second pushes — Discord ticks `timestamps.start` client-side.
- **Connections:** userdoccers notes a per-client limit of **2 connections/min** — so reconnect with backoff, don't spin.
- **Reconnection:** the socket dies when Discord quits/restarts. The updater must detect EOF/write errors, then retry connecting across `discord-ipc-0..9` with exponential backoff, re-handshake, and re-send the last activity. Both recommended crates surface connect/set errors as `Result`.

### 6. Rust crate comparison & recommendation

| Crate | Latest | Released | Model | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **discord-rich-presence (vionya)** | **1.1.0** | 2026-01-26 | **sync** | 330k dl / 119k recent. Clean builder (`Activity`, `Assets`, `Timestamps`, `Party`, `Button`). Cross-platform, same API on Unix/Windows. **Recommended.** |
| discord-presence (jewlexx) | 3.2.0 | 2026-01-20 | event-driven (threads, crossbeam/parking_lot) | 86k dl. Has `event_handler`, models, connection module → built-in events/reconnect helpers. Heavier; good if you want event callbacks. |
| discord-sdk (Embark) | 0.4.0 | 2024-12-17 | async | **Stale (2024)**, wraps the **deprecated** Discord Game SDK. Avoid. |

**Recommendation: `discord-rich-presence` v1.1.0.** It is the lightest, most-used, and most maintained, with a direct mapping to every field we need. Because it's sync, run it on a dedicated thread (or `spawn_blocking` from Tokio) and feed it state over a channel; that thread owns connect/throttle/reconnect.

Verified builder signatures (from the crate source):
```rust
use discord_rich_presence::{activity::*, DiscordIpc, DiscordIpcClient};

let mut client = DiscordIpcClient::new("<APPLICATION_ID>")?;
client.connect()?;

let activity = Activity::new()
    .details("Running command cargo check - private (master)") // <=128
    .state("Opus 4.8 | Pro - $0.98 - 837.7K tok - Ctx 33%")    // <=128
    .timestamps(Timestamps::new().start(turn_started_unix))     // start: i64 -> elapsed timer
    .party(Party::new().id("claude-sessions").size([2, 4]))     // [current, max] -> "2 of 4" agents
    .assets(
        Assets::new()
            .large_image("https://example.com/claude.png")      // external URL OK, no upload
            .large_text("private")
            .small_image("opus")                                // or asset key
            .small_text("in focus"),
    )
    .buttons(vec![Button::new("View project", "https://github.com/...")]);

client.set_activity(activity)?;
// ...
client.clear_activity()?; // or set_activity(Activity::new()) / send activity: null
```
Method facts confirmed from `src/activity.rs`: `Party::size(self, [i32; 2])`, `Timestamps::start(self, i64)`, `Assets::{large_image,large_text,small_image,small_text}`, `Button::new(label, url)`, `Activity::{state,details,assets,timestamps,party,buttons}`.

### 7. Codex screenshot → Activity mapping (the whole point)

| Codex screenshot element | Activity field | Source |
|---|---|---|
| "Codex App" header | application **name** ("Claude Code") | app registration |
| "Running command cargo check - dinero (master)" | **details** | current tool/Bash cmd + cwd basename + git branch |
| "GPT-5.5 (Medium) | Pro ($200/mo) - $0.980 - 837.7K tok - Ctx 33%" | **state** | model + plan + cost + tokens + ctx% from transcript usage |
| elapsed "0:06" | **timestamps.start** | start of current turn/command |
| app icon | **assets.large_image** (+ small_image overlay) | external URL or asset key |
| (user's extra) # agents running | **party.size [n, max]** | count of live sessions (2 right now on this machine) |
| (user's extra) project in focus | **assets.small_text** / details branch part | focused session's cwd |

All seven Codex fields map cleanly, and the two user extras (agent count, focused project) fit `party.size` and the assets hover text. The only field that needs care is `state` — it's a tight 128-char budget, so model/plan strings must be abbreviated.

### Sources
- IPC protocol & opcodes: https://docs.discord.food/topics/rpc
- Activity object schema & limits: https://docs.discord.food/resources/presence
- External image URLs / setting RP: https://docs.discord.com/developers/discord-social-sdk/development-guides/setting-rich-presence
- Rate-limit reality: https://github.com/discord/discord-api-docs/issues/668 ; https://github.com/discord/discord-rpc/blob/master/documentation/hard-mode.md
- Crates: https://crates.io/crates/discord-rich-presence (v1.1.0) ; https://crates.io/crates/discord-presence (v3.2.0) ; https://crates.io/crates/discord-sdk (v0.4.0) ; src https://github.com/vionya/discord-rich-presence

## Lane C2 — Discord Rich Presence: PROVEN END-TO-END ON THIS MACHINE

**Bottom line: it works, twice over.** A raw stdlib python3 client and the Rust `discord-rich-presence` crate both completed the full HANDSHAKE → SET_ACTIVITY → CLEAR cycle against the live Discord IPC socket. This single result clears the biggest feasibility risk for the project.

### Environment verified (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE)
- Socket: `srwxr-xr-x discord-ipc-0` at `$TMPDIR` = `/var/folders/3y/gmkcmkk1459813sgvwl32q700000gn/T/`
- Discord.app running (pid 87768)
- `python3` 3.14.4 (`/opt/homebrew/bin/python3`), `cargo` 1.95.0 (`~/.cargo/bin/cargo`)
- 2 live Claude Code sessions: pid 98608 (cwd `…/private/fnetflyx`, `--resume ff489433…`) and pid 98863 (cwd `…/private`), both `--model claude-opus-4-8`

### The client_id
Public, non-secret: **`383226320970055681`** — the canonical VS Code RPC app id, taken from `export const CLIENT_ID = '383226320970055681'` in [iCrawl/discord-vscode/src/constants.ts](https://github.com/iCrawl/discord-vscode/blob/master/src/constants.ts). On this machine Discord resolved it to `name: "Visual Studio Code"`. For the real product, register a free Discord application to get a custom "Claude Code" name + icon.

### Actual Discord responses (raw)
**HANDSHAKE → READY** (proves id is valid + identifies the real user):
```json
{ "cmd":"DISPATCH","evt":"READY","nonce":null,
  "data":{ "v":1,"config":{"environment":"production",...},
    "user":{"id":"1268624384784863326","username":"qxp_dc","global_name":"Jay",...} } }
```
**SET_ACTIVITY → success** (matching nonce, `evt:null` = no error, fields echoed):
```json
{ "cmd":"SET_ACTIVITY","evt":null,"nonce":"nonce-1781989303927",
  "data":{ "details":"2 Claude Code sessions","state":"Opus 4.8 | feasibility PoC",
    "timestamps":{"start":1781989303000},"party":{"id":"claude-agents","size":[2,5]},
    "name":"Visual Studio Code","application_id":"383226320970055681","type":0 } }
```
**CLEAR (activity=null) → data:null**, socket closed, no stale presence:
```json
{ "cmd":"SET_ACTIVITY","data":null,"evt":null,"nonce":"nonce-clear-1781989306934" }
```

### The working python3 script (stdlib only)
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import socket, struct, json, os, time

CLIENT_ID = "383226320970055681"  # public VS Code app id (iCrawl/discord-vscode)

def ipc_path():
    base = os.environ.get("XDG_RUNTIME_DIR") or os.environ.get("TMPDIR") or "/tmp"
    return os.path.join(base, "discord-ipc-0")

def send(sock, op, payload):
    data = json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")
    sock.sendall(struct.pack("<II", op, len(data)) + data)

def recv(sock):
    header = b""
    while len(header) < 8:
        chunk = sock.recv(8 - len(header))
        if not chunk: raise ConnectionError("closed")
        header += chunk
    op, length = struct.unpack("<II", header)
    body = b""
    while len(body) < length:
        chunk = sock.recv(length - len(body))
        if not chunk: raise ConnectionError("closed")
        body += chunk
    return op, json.loads(body.decode("utf-8"))

def main():
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX, socket.SOCK_STREAM); s.settimeout(10)
    s.connect(ipc_path())
    send(s, 0, {"v": 1, "client_id": CLIENT_ID})          # HANDSHAKE
    print(json.dumps(recv(s)[1], indent=2))                # expect evt:READY
    nonce = f"nonce-{int(time.time()*1000)}"
    activity = {"details":"2 Claude Code sessions","state":"Opus 4.8 | feasibility PoC",
                "timestamps":{"start":int(time.time())},
                "party":{"id":"claude-agents","size":[2,5]}}
    send(s, 1, {"cmd":"SET_ACTIVITY","nonce":nonce,
                "args":{"pid":os.getpid(),"activity":activity}})
    print(json.dumps(recv(s)[1], indent=2))                # success if nonce matches, evt!=ERROR
    time.sleep(3)
    n2 = f"nonce-clear-{int(time.time()*1000)}"
    send(s, 1, {"cmd":"SET_ACTIVITY","nonce":n2,
                "args":{"pid":os.getpid(),"activity":None}})  # CLEAR
    print(json.dumps(recv(s)[1], indent=2))
    s.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
Frame format confirmed working: `struct.pack('<II', op, len(json_bytes)) + json_bytes`; op=0 handshake, op=1 frame, response op=1.

### Rust PoC (bonus) — also PASS
`cargo run` on a temp project with `discord-rich-presence = "0.2"` and client_id `383226320970055681` printed `RUST: connected` / `RUST: set_activity OK` / `RUST: cleared + closed OK`, exit 0. The crate API used:
```rust

```
This confirms the Rust path the user prefers is viable with a mature, working crate — no need to hand-roll the IPC protocol in Rust (though it's trivial if desired).

### Where every desired RP field comes from (data plumbing)
All sources below are VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE unless noted:
- **details** (`Running cargo check - dinero (master)`): `cwd` + `gitBranch` from the transcript JSONL (`cwd=/Users/jakubsladek/Projects/private`, `version=2.1.181`); activity verb from latest tool_use / assistant event, or the `aiTitle` label (`"Analyze Claude rich presence implementation feasibility"`). Note: gitBranch was `HEAD` (detached) here — fall back to `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`.
- **state** (`Opus 4.8 | Pro | $0.98 | 837K tok | Ctx 33%`): model from transcript / process `--model claude-opus-4-8`; tokens from `usage` (`input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`, `output_tokens`); **cost is the one gap** — compute from tokens × pricing (ccusage logic; ccusage not installed but `npx` is). Plan string ($/month) is not in local state → user-config constant. Ctx% = used input+cache tokens ÷ model context window (200K for Opus, a documented constant).
- **timestamps.start** (elapsed timer): process `lstart` or first transcript timestamp; Discord ticks it client-side (verified accepted).
- **party.size** (running agents): live process count = **2**, via `ps` filtered to `claude.app/Contents/MacOS/claude` excluding `--type=` helpers and the `disclaimer` wrapper; per-session cwd via `lsof -a -p <pid> -d cwd`. This directly answers "how many agents running / which project".
- **large_image / name / icon**: the developer's own registered Discord app.

### Important product constraint
Discord exposes **one Rich Presence per app id per user**. Multiple sessions cannot each show their own presence under one app — they must be aggregated into a single activity (party.size = N, details = the focused session). Picking "which session is in focus" needs a heuristic (most-recently-active transcript by mtime, or frontmost terminal).

### Cleanup
Both PoCs cleared activity (`data:null`) and closed their sockets; temp files removed. The `discord-ipc-0` socket node remains because Discord owns it (expected) — no leftover presence on the profile.

investigations.feasibility_summary: Highly feasible, and largely a "study the prior art and improve it" exercise rather than greenfield R&D. The exact Codex screenshot the user shared is reproducible — `xt0n1-t3ch/Codex-Discord-Rich-Presence` already renders that identical composite (model, plan, cost, tokens, context %, quota, elapsed) in Rust over Discord IPC, and at least three Claude-Code-specific tools already exist (`StealthyLabsHQ/claude-rpc` is Rust/Tauri; `tsanva/cc-discord-presence` is Go with the richest field set via a statusline-wrapper hook; plus several mature VSCode extensions). The sanctioned mechanism is a registered Discord application + the local IPC socket via a crate like `discord-rich-presence` (vionya), which exposes `set_activity`/`clear_activity`/`reconnect`. The Discord custom-status-via-user-token route is a self-bot, violates ToS, and must NOT be used. Main non-trivial design problems are well-mapped: idle handling, the ~15s rate limit, multi-session "which one wins", reconnect on Discord restart, asset/icon naming, and privacy (don't leak prompt text or filenames).

Live verification of the IPC socket, JSONL schema, multi-session detection and the discord-rich-presence crate connecting belongs to the implementation/live-test lanes; this lane supplies the design map and exact crate/client_id/field references for them.

risks: Discord ~15s/update rate limit: a naive 'update on every JSONL line' loop will be throttled and presence will lag or get dropped — must debounce/coalesce to newest and republish on a fixed cadence (Codex-RP republishes every ~2s to hold stack priority; that is borderline and relies on coalescing, validate against current Discord behavior).

Self-bot temptation: a 'real' free-text custom status (not the app-scoped Rich Presence) requires a user token and is an explicit ToS violation with account-ban risk. Hard rule: do NOT implement the user-token path; ship only the registered-application Rich Presence path.

Privacy leakage: JSONL transcripts contain prompt text, file paths, and possibly secrets. details/state lines and tooltips must be sanitized — show project slug + branch + a coarse activity verb, never raw prompt content or full file paths. Provide a privacy/whitelist mode (vscord app.privacyMode.enable / app.whitelist pattern).

Token/cost/context accuracy: pure-JSONL estimation drifts from Claude's real numbers; the reliable source is the statusLine hook. But hijacking the user's statusLine command can clobber an existing custom statusline — must wrap/chain it, not overwrite (cc-discord-presence ships statusline-wrapper.sh exactly to chain).

Multi-session flicker / 'which wins': two live sessions competing for one Discord presence will thrash without a stickiness window and a clear selection rule (most-recently-active JSONL). Need stale-cutoff + active-sticky timers (Codex CODEX_PRESENCE_STALE_SECONDS / ACTIVE_STICKY_SECONDS).

Asset management overhead: every icon (logo, model tiers, language icons, idle badge) must be pre-uploaded to the Developer Portal under our app id; adding icons later requires portal edits, not just code. Plan the asset key set up front.

Reconnect on Discord restart: if Discord quits/restarts the IPC socket dies; the app must detect send failure and call reconnect() with backoff (crate supports reconnect()), otherwise presence silently stops. iCrawl/vscord both had recurring 'couldn't connect to Discord via RPC' issues — connection lifecycle is the most common failure mode in the field.

Plan/quota % availability: usage-limit percentages (5h/7d, Ctx %) may not be reliably exposed by Claude Code's local state (claude-rpc notes limits are cached and need manual refresh); some screenshot-parity fields may be approximate or stale.
open_questions: Does Claude Code's statusLine command receive model + cost + token + context-% in its stdin JSON the way cc-discord-presence assumes (Claude-native numbers)? Needs a live check of what Claude pipes to the statusLine command on this v2.1.181 — that determines whether we get authoritative state vs estimates. (Implementation/live lane.)

What exactly is in the JSONL usage fields per assistant turn (cumulative vs delta tokens, cost present or computed)? Drives the JSONL-fallback accuracy. (Live lane to dump a transcript line, redacted.)
Liveness/heuristic for 'session is running': process-presence (PIDs ~98608/98863 noted) vs JSONL mtime freshness — which is robust when a session is idle-but-open? (Live lane.)
Should we register ONE 'Claude Code' Discord app or multiple for surface-aware switching (Code/Desktop/sub-agent)? The Codex tool uses 2 app ids; decide product-side.
Current Discord coalescing behavior for sub-15s SET_ACTIVITY over IPC (vs the old SDK) — confirm whether a ~2s republish cadence is safe today before copying Codex-RP's cadence.
investigations.report_markdown:
## D1 — Prior art & design lessons: Discord Rich Presence for Claude Code

### TL;DR
This is **not greenfield**. The exact Codex screenshot the user shared is produced by an existing Rust tool, and there are already ≥3 Claude-Code-specific Discord presence tools plus a decade of battle-tested VSCode extensions to mine. Our job is mostly: pick the cleanest data source (a `statusLine` hook + JSONL fallback), reuse the `discord-rich-presence` Rust crate for IPC, register our own Discord app for the name/icon, and get the **lifecycle** details right (idle, rate-limit batching, reconnect, multi-session selection, privacy).

---

### 1) VSCode Discord presence extensions — the canonical pattern

| Repo | Lib | Default client_id | Idle config |
|---|---|---|---|
| `leonardssh/vscord` (modern, 130+ langs, 60+ settings) | `@xhayper/discord-rpc ^1.3.4` | `782685898163617802` | `status.idle.timeout` default **300s**, plus `status.idle.enabled/check/disconnectOnIdle/resetElapsedTime` |
| `iCrawl/discord-vscode` (the original "Discord Presence") | `discord-rpc ^4.0.1` | `383226320970055681` (hardcoded in `src/constants.ts`: `export const CLIENT_ID = '383226320970055681'`) | `discord.idleTimeout` default **0** (0 = never clear) |

**How they get state:** VSCode API for active file (name/ext/size/relative path), workspace name, and the built-in `vscode.git` extension for branch/owner/remote. Language → icon is a **lookup table of 130+ asset keys** (`{lang}`/`{Lang}`/`{LANG}` variables in vscord). 

**How they push:** a JS Discord-RPC client over the local IPC socket; `setActivity()` on `ready`. The maintained library today is **`@xhayper/discord-rpc`** (fork of the deprecated `discord-rpc`).

**Idle:** timer since last edit/selection; on timeout either switch to an "idle" presence or `disconnectOnIdle`. vscord can `resetElapsedTime` on idle so the timer doesn't lie.

**Privacy:** `app.privacyMode.enable`, `app.whitelist[]` / `whitelistIsBlacklist`, and `ignore.workspaces/repositories/organizations/gitHosts` — i.e. you can hide specific repos/orgs from ever appearing. **We should copy this verbatim** since JSONL is even more sensitive than filenames.

**Reconnection** is the field's #1 pain point (vscord issues #215, #52, #364 are all "couldn't connect to Discord via RPC"). Lesson: treat the IPC connection as flaky; detect send failure and reconnect with backoff.

### 2) Existing Claude Code / Codex / terminal presence tools (directly reusable prior art)

- **`StealthyLabsHQ/claude-rpc`** — *the closest architectural match to our target*: **native Tauri/Rust** system-tray app (Rust ~76%), Discord IPC in Rust, **250 ms** detection polling, reads `~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl`, `/model` fallback, provider detection (env/settings/API-key/Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry/OAuth), tray controls, config toggles `showLimits/showLimit5h/showLimitAll/showLimitSonnet/showLimitDesign/showProvider/showEffort/showSessionTitle/rpcMode/buttons`. Caveat: usage-limit %s are cached and need a manual Usage-page refresh.
- **`tsanva/cc-discord-presence`** — *the richest field set* (Go): shows **project + git branch, model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku 4.5), total tokens, session cost $, elapsed**. Two data sources: (a) JSONL fallback parsing `~/.claude/projects/`, and (b) preferred **statusLine hook** — it copies `statusline-wrapper.sh` to `~/.claude/` and sets `settings.json` → `"statusLine": { "command": "~/.claude/statusline-wrapper.sh", "type": "command" }` to capture Claude's *own* cost/token/context numbers (logs `using statusline data` vs `using JSONL fallback`). Uses `fsnotify` for file watching. Default shared app "Clawd Code"; custom id via `ClientID` in `main.go`. **This hook trick is the single most valuable idea to steal** for accurate state.
- **`xt0n1-t3ch/Codex-Discord-Rich-Presence`** — *reproduces the user's exact screenshot*, in **Rust**, native macOS arm64. State composite: `⚡ GPT-5.5 | Pro ($200/month) • $7.13 • 31.5M tok • Ctx 19% used • 5h 100% • 7d 100%`; details `Running command cargo test`. Reads `~/.codex/` JSONL + OpenCode SQLite. **Surface-aware switching via multiple Discord app ids**: `Codex` CLI/VSCode `1470480085453770854` (asset `codex-logo`) vs `Codex App` `1478395304624652345` (asset `codex-app`). Tunables: `CODEX_PRESENCE_POLL_SECONDS`, `CODEX_PRESENCE_STALE_SECONDS`, `CODEX_PRESENCE_ACTIVE_STICKY_SECONDS`; republishes every ~2s to hold stack priority; `status`/`doctor` health checks. Config `~/.codex/discord-presence-config.json`.
- **`Puri12/opencode-discord-presence`** — npm/OpenCode plugin (TS, `@xhayper/discord-rpc`): event-driven (chat.message/tool.execute/file.edited/todo.updated/session.idle). Notable options: **`mainAgentOnly`** (filter sub-agent sessions so background planners don't flicker the presence) and card rotation ("critical states pin; informational cards rotate every 20s"). Good multi-agent UX precedent.

**Rust crate to build on:** `discord-rich-presence` (vionya). `DiscordIpc` trait exposes `connect, reconnect, close, set_activity, clear_activity, send_handshake, send, recv`. **`reconnect()` exists** ("closes the active connection, then re-connects and re-sends a handshake", `Result<(),Error>`) — this is exactly the resilience primitive the JS extensions had to hand-roll. Cross-platform (Unix socket on macOS). Example:
```rust
let mut client = DiscordIpcClient::new("<our client id>")?;
client.connect()?;
client.set_activity(activity::Activity::new()
    .details("Editing src/main.rs — private (master)")
    .state("Opus 4.8 | Max | $0.98 • 837.7K tok • Ctx 33%")
    .timestamps(activity::Timestamps::new().start(session_start_epoch))
    .assets(activity::Assets::new().large_image("claude-logo"))
    .party(activity::Party::new().size([1, n_sessions])))?;
```

### 3) Alternatives to Discord RP

| Approach | When it beats Discord RP | Trade-off |
|---|---|---|
| **Discord Rich Presence (app + IPC)** | Default. Matches the screenshot 1:1; native ticking timer, icon, two text lines, party size. | Requires Discord running + registered app; ~15s rate limit; only shows on *your* profile to people who share a server/friendship. |
| **Discord custom-status via user token (self-bot)** | Only way to get free-text status without an app. | **ToS violation / self-bot → account ban risk. NOT RECOMMENDED — do not build.** (Discord Support "Automated User Accounts": automating user accounts outside OAuth2/bot API is forbidden, "can result in account termination".) |
| **Lanyard** (`Phineas/lanyard`) | You want the data *readable elsewhere* — web dashboard, GitHub-profile badge, mobile. It re-exposes the very Rich Presence we set, over REST+WS. | Read-side only (doesn't *set* presence); needs membership in its Discord server; self-host for privacy. **Complements** Discord RP, doesn't replace it. |
| **macOS menu-bar app** (Rust `tray-icon` + `tao`, or Tauri like claude-rpc) | You want *local-first*, no Discord dependency, richer multi-session list, controls. | No social surface; you build all the UI. Best as the *host* for the RP updater (tray = where claude-rpc already lives). |
| **Slack status API** (`users.profile.set`) | Your team lives in Slack, not Discord. | Single short text + emoji only; no timer/party/icon richness; per-workspace token. |
| **Local web dashboard** | Internal multi-machine/agent overview, no per-viewer Discord requirement. | You host + secure it; no "presence on my gamer profile" vibe. |

**Recommendation:** ship Discord Rich Presence as primary (a registered "Claude Code" app), host it inside a macOS menu-bar/Tauri process (so it can also show the multi-session list and controls), and optionally surface the same data via Lanyard for a web/README badge.

### 4) Concrete design lessons for OUR app

1. **Data source priority:** prefer a **statusLine-wrapper hook** (Claude's authoritative model/cost/tokens/context) and **chain, don't overwrite** any existing `statusLine.command`; fall back to JSONL tail parsing of `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>.jsonl`. (cc-discord-presence pattern.)
2. **Idle:** track last-activity from JSONL mtime; after a timeout (default ~300s like vscord) switch to an "idle" presence or `clear_activity()`; optionally `resetElapsedTime` so the timer doesn't claim hours of fake work. Make timeout configurable; 0 = never clear (iCrawl default).
3. **Rate-limit batching:** Discord coalesces to ~1 update/15s. **Debounce to "newest wins"** and republish on a fixed cadence rather than on every JSONL line. Don't assume sub-2s updates land.
4. **Reconnect on Discord restart:** wrap every `set_activity` send; on error, `reconnect()` with exponential backoff. This is the most common field failure (every VSCode ext has the "couldn't connect via RPC" issue). The Rust crate gives you `reconnect()` for free.
5. **Multi-instance / which-session-wins:** one Discord profile = one presence. Select the **most-recently-active session by JSONL mtime**, apply a **stale cutoff** and an **active-sticky window** to stop flicker (Codex-RP's `STALE_SECONDS`/`ACTIVE_STICKY_SECONDS`). Encode the rest as **`party.size = [1, N]`** ("2 of 3 sessions") to satisfy the user's "how many agents running" ask. Consider an `mainAgentOnly`-style filter (opencode) so background sub-agents don't steal focus.
6. **Asset management:** pre-upload a fixed icon set (Claude logo as `large_image`, optional model-tier/idle/language badges as `small_image`) to the app's Art Assets in the Developer Portal; you reference them by **key**, so plan the set up front. Surface-aware name/icon = swap **client_id** (Codex uses 2 app ids).
7. **Privacy (non-negotiable, JSONL is sensitive):** never put raw prompt text, full file paths, or secrets in `details`/`state`/tooltips. Show project slug + branch + a coarse activity verb. Ship a **privacy mode + repo whitelist/blacklist** (vscord `app.privacyMode.enable`, `app.whitelist`, `ignore.*`). Redact before send.

### Key source URLs
- Claude Code prior art: github.com/StealthyLabsHQ/claude-rpc · github.com/tsanva/cc-discord-presence · github.com/Puri12/opencode-discord-presence
- Codex (screenshot-parity): github.com/xt0n1-t3ch/Codex-Discord-Rich-Presence
- VSCode pattern: github.com/leonardssh/vscord (src/config.ts, package.json) · github.com/iCrawl/discord-vscode (src/constants.ts)
- Rust crate: crates.io/crates/discord-rich-presence · docs.rs/discord-rich-presence trait.DiscordIpc.html · github.com/vionya/discord-rich-presence
- Alternatives: github.com/Phineas/lanyard
- Constraints: discord/discord-api-docs issue #668 (15s rate limit) · Discord Support "Automated User Accounts (Self-Bots)" · Discord docs "Setting Rich Presence"

# VERIFY V3 — Independent re-check (multi-session / agent-count / focus)

All checks below were run live by me on this Mac at probe time. Verdict: **confirmed** — the prior lanes' core enumeration, mapping, and subagent claims reproduce, with two evidence-strength corrections (pgrep is worse than 'intermittent'; CPU is not a reliable busy signal).

## 1. Engine enumeration — CONFIRMED (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE, high)
- Exactly **2** live engines: `98608` (cwd `.../private/fnetflyx`) and `98863` (cwd `.../private`).
- Each engine's parent is a `/Applications/Claude.app/Contents/Helpers/disclaimer` wrapper (`98607->98608`, `98862->98863`); engines are NOT children of each other.
- Engine binary: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code/2.1.181/claude.app/Contents/MacOS/claude`.
- **Robust enumeration** = filter on comm executable path: `ps -axww -o pid=,comm= | awk '/claude-code\\// && /\\/MacOS\\/claude$/'`. Returned `98608 98863` cleanly on 5/5 samples; excludes the disclaimer wrapper and the capital-C `/Applications/Claude.app/.../MacOS/Claude` GUI.

### pgrep correction (stronger than B1 claimed)
`pgrep -f 'claude-code'` returned `98607 98608` on **5/5** consecutive samples — it missed engine `98863` 100% of the time and always included the disclaimer wrapper `98607`. Not flaky; deterministically wrong here. **Do not use pgrep -f.** Use comm-path filtering (Rust: `sysinfo` `process.exe()`, never truncated).

## 2. 'N agents running' definition — CONFIRMED (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE, high)
- Authoritative source = `~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json`. 2 files, both PIDs alive (`os.kill(pid,0)`), no stale entries.
- Each file: `{pid, sessionId, cwd, startedAt, procStart, version, peerProtocol, kind:"interactive", entrypoint:"claude-desktop"}`.
- **Three independent counts all = 2** (comm-filter / live-registry / total-registry).
- Recommended metric: `total_sessions` = registry files whose PID is alive; `busy_sessions` = those whose transcript tail is BUSY (see §4). Discord `party.size = [busy, total]`.
- Note: registry files can in principle outlive a crashed process, so always gate on `kill(pid,0)` — here there were zero stale entries, but the guard is mandatory.

## 3. PID -> cwd -> transcript — CONFIRMED (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE, high)
- `lsof -a -p <pid> -d cwd -Fn` cwd matched registry cwd exactly for both.
- cwd -> slug (`/`->`-`) -> newest-mtime `.jsonl` == registry `sessionId` for both (`MATCH=True`), and an **exact-named** `<sessionId>.jsonl` exists for both. Either mapping works; exact-name is more direct since the registry already gives the sessionId.
- argv is **stale**: `98608` argv shows `--resume ff489433...` but the resident session is `27c2524d...` (forked). Trust the registry sessionId, never argv.

## 4. Active vs idle — CONFIRMED logic, CPU demoted (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE, high)
Heuristic: parse transcript filtering to `type in {user,assistant}` with dict `message`; track pending = `tool_use` ids minus `tool_result` `tool_use_id`s. `pending>0` => BUSY(mid-tool); else last==assistant => IDLE; else last==user => BUSY(awaiting assistant). Live result:
- `98608` => BUSY(mid-tool, pending=1), tr_age 12s, **cpu 0.1%**
- `98863` => IDLE(assistant done), tr_age 669s, **cpu 2.9%**

**Correction:** B1/B3 called CPU% 'the strong live signal'. At my instant CPU was inverted (BUSY 0.1% < IDLE 2.9%). The transcript pending-tool-call heuristic is the reliable signal; treat CPU as an optional weak corroborator only. Also must skip trailing `last-prompt`/`ai-title`/`mode` records (no message body).

## 5. Subagent counting (adversarial) — CONFIRMED in-process, not OS procs (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE, high)
- Children of engines are ONLY MCP servers: `npm exec @playwright/mcp@latest` (98616 under 98608; 98910 under 98863). No child `MacOS/claude`. So Task/Explore/Workflow subagents are **in-process**, never separate processes — a process-based subagent count is impossible and would always read 0.
- On-disk surface: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl` (`isSidechain:true`, `agentId`, parent `sessionId`, `gitBranch`, `cwd`) + sidecar `agent-<id>.meta.json = {agentType, description}` (verified: `{"agentType":"Explore","description":"Backend security audit (Rust/Axum)"}`).
- **169** sidechain transcript files exist historically; **0** modified in the last 60 min at probe time. So a live-subagent metric = count of sidechain files with recent mtime under the active session's dir; right now that's 0. (B3's 653 is a line count, not a file/live count.)

## 6. Focus detection inside Claude.app — CONFIRMED limited (VERIFIED-ON-MACHINE, high)
- `lsappinfo front` -> `LSDisplayName="Claude"`, no permission prompt. Frontmost app NAME/bundle is free (Rust: `NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication`).
- Both engines have tty `??` (no controlling terminal) -> tty-based terminal-window mapping is impossible for the Claude.app-spawned setup.
- `CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess = False` -> window TITLES are gated behind Screen Recording; combined with B2's finding that Claude.app's AX tree is a single window titled "Claude" with no per-session/tab labels, **true per-session OS focus is not obtainable**. Ship 'focus' = most-recently-active session by transcript mtime (and optionally the registry sessionId whose transcript has the freshest mtime). Terminal-title parsing only helps if the user runs `claude` in a real terminal, which is not the current setup.

## Net recommendation for the RP app
- Enumerate engines by comm path (sysinfo `exe()`), NOT pgrep.
- Drive everything off `~/.claude/sessions/<PID>.json` (gate on `kill(pid,0)`): gives pid, sessionId, cwd, entrypoint for free, and the exact transcript filename.
- Per session: project = last path component of cwd; gitBranch + cost/token/model from the same transcript; busy/idle from pending-tool-call heuristic.
- `party.size = [busy_sessions, total_sessions]`; live-subagent badge = recent-mtime sidechain files; focus = freshest-mtime session.
- CPU only as a soft tiebreaker, never the busy authority.
